plugins.name: ask-questions-if-underspecified
plugins.description: Clarify ambiguous requirements by asking questions before implementing. Only when invoked explicitly.

plugins.name: avoid-ai-writing
plugins.description: Audit and rewrite content to remove AI writing patterns ("AI-isms"). Use this skill when asked to "remove AI-isms," "clean up AI writing," "edit writing for AI patterns," "audit writing for AI tells," or "make this sound less like AI." Supports a detection-only mode that flags patterns without rewriting.

plugins.name: careful
plugins.description: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations. User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems, or working in a shared environment.

plugins.name: claude-vis
plugins.description: Logs session activity, token usage, and cost to SQLite for analytics and visualization

plugins.name: devcontainer-setup
plugins.description: Create pre-configured devcontainers with Claude Code and language-specific tooling

plugins.name: differential-review
plugins.description: Security-focused differential review of code changes with git history analysis and blast radius estimation

plugins.name: file-organizer
plugins.description: Intelligently organizes your files and folders across your computer by understanding context, finding duplicates, suggesting better structures, and automating cleanup tasks. Reduces cognitive load and keeps your digital workspace tidy without manual effort.

plugins.name: git-cleanup
plugins.description: Safely analyzes and cleans up local git branches and worktrees by categorizing them as merged, squash-merged, superseded, or active work.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".

plugins.name: insecure-defaults
plugins.description: Detects insecure default configurations including hardcoded credentials, fallback secrets, weak authentication defaults, and dangerous values in production

plugins.name: investigate
plugins.description: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate, analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause. Use when asked to debug, fix a bug, or do root cause analysis.

plugins.name: modern-python
plugins.description: Modern Python best practices. Use when creating new Python projects, and writing Python scripts, or migrating existing projects from legacy tools.

plugins.name: office-hours
plugins.description: YC Office Hours with two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation, and future-fit. Builder mode: design thinking brainstorming for side projects, hackathons, learning, and open source.

plugins.name: review
plugins.description: Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues.

plugins.name: semgrep-rule-creator
plugins.description: Create custom Semgrep rules for detecting bug patterns and security vulnerabilities

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Ship workflow: detect and merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR.

plugins.name: variant-analysis
plugins.description: Find similar vulnerabilities and bugs across codebases using pattern-based analysis

plugins.name: workflow-skill-design
plugins.description: Teaches design patterns for workflow-based Claude Code skills and provides a review agent for auditing existing skills

plugins.name: yt-transcript
plugins.description: Fetch YouTube video transcripts and save them as clean Markdown notes.
